Common causes and conditions that can lead to poor television or radio reception include the following:

1. Ignition or electrical interference
The picture may flutter, drift, show black spots, or have horizontal streaks. This is usually caused by nearby electrical interference, such as automobile ignition systems, neon lamps, electrical drills, or other household electrical appliances.

<PIC>

2. Ghosting or multiple images
Ghosts occur when the broadcast signal reaches the TV through more than one path. One signal comes directly from the transmitter, while another is reflected by tall buildings, hills, or other large objects. This can create a doubled or shadow-like image. Adjusting the antenna direction or position may help improve reception.

<PIC>

3. Snow or speckled picture
Snow appears as many small dots across the screen. This often happens when the receiver is in a weak-signal or fringe reception area. In this case, the antenna signal may not be strong enough, and using a better-positioned or special outdoor antenna may improve the picture.

<PIC>

Other possible causes include:
- An old, damaged, or poor-quality recorded tape is being played.
- Strong random interference from a car, airplane, or other nearby signal source affects the TV signal.
- The antenna signal is weak or unstable.
- The program signal itself may not contain proper caption or broadcast information.
- Poor antenna/cable connections, weathered antenna cables, or an incorrectly positioned antenna can also reduce reception quality.

If you are experiencing poor reception, you can try these checks:
1. Try another channel to confirm whether the issue is with one station only.
2. Check that the antenna or cable connection is firmly attached.
3. Check for nearby sources of local interference, such as electrical appliances or motors.
4. Adjust the antenna direction or position.
5. Run AUTO PROGRAM again to search and store available channels.
6. If the picture has horizontal stripes, distortion, or unstable color, use the Fine adjustment function for the affected channel.

<PIC>,['television0_01', 'television0_02', 'television0_03', 'Manual35_45']

Closed captioning displays the audio portion of a TV program as written text on the screen, similar to subtitles. You can turn captions or text services on and choose the display mode in either of the following two ways.

<PIC>

## Option 1: Use the CAPTION button on the remote
This is the quickest way to switch caption/text modes while watching TV.

1. Press the **CAPTION** button on the remote control.
2. Press the **CAPTION** button repeatedly to cycle through the available options:
   - **OFF**: Turns captions/text off.
   - **Mode1**: Usually the main closed-caption signal for program dialogue and narration.
   - **Mode2**: May show a secondary caption signal, such as demonstration or programming information.
   - **Text1 / Text2**: Displays text-service information on the screen when available.
3. When your preferred mode is shown, press **ENTER (O)** to exit.

<PIC>

## Option 2: Use the on-screen MENU
Use this method if you prefer changing the setting through the TV’s menu.

1. Press the **MENU** button.
2. Use the **up/down** buttons to select the **Special** menu.
3. Press the **right** button to enter the Special menu.
4. Use the **up/down** buttons to highlight **Caption**.
5. Press the **right** button again, then use the **up/down** buttons to choose one of the following:
   - **Off**
   - **Mode1**
   - **Mode2**
   - **Text1**
   - **Text2**
6. Press the **MENU** button repeatedly to close the menu and return to normal viewing.

<PIC>

## Helpful notes
- **Mode1** is usually the standard caption mode used for dialogue and narration.
- **Mode2** may carry alternate information, depending on the broadcast.
- **Text1** and **Text2** can display full-screen text information, such as program lists, weather, news, or other station-provided content.
- Not every TV program or station provides closed captions or text services.
- If the broadcast signal is weak or distorted, captions or text may not display correctly.
- The TV remembers the last caption/text mode you selected, even after the TV is turned off.,['Manual35_36', 'Manual35_37', 'Manual35_38']

For optimal signal reception, we recommend using an outdoor antenna. Before connecting it, please inspect the antenna and cable carefully—severe weathering, corrosion, loose fittings, or damaged cable can reduce signal quality.

1. Connect the outdoor antenna to the TV

Please choose the connection method that matches your antenna cable:

- 300 Ohm flat wire
  1. Attach the 300 Ohm flat wire to the screw terminals on a 300 Ohm-to-75 Ohm adapter.
  2. Insert the adapter into the TV’s 75 Ohm antenna jack.

- 75 Ohm coaxial cable
  1. Connect the 75 Ohm coaxial cable directly to the TV’s 75 Ohm antenna jack.

The diagram below shows both connection types and the correct location of the 75 Ohm antenna jack on the rear of the TV.

<PIC>

2. Run Auto Program after the antenna is connected

After the antenna is connected securely, turn on the TV and let it search for available channels using AUTO PROGRAM. This allows the TV to memorize all active channels in your area.

Using the AUTO PRG. button on the remote control:
1. Press the AUTO PRG. button.
2. Press the AUTO PRG. button again to start AUTO PROGRAM.
3. The TV will begin searching and storing available channels automatically.

If you need to stop the search before it finishes, press the ENTER button. Please note that only the channels found up to that point will be stored.

<PIC>

<PIC>

3. Adjust the antenna direction if reception is poor

If the picture is unclear, unstable, or shows double images, try slowly changing the antenna’s direction or position. Ghosting usually happens when the TV signal reaches the antenna through more than one path—for example, one direct signal and another reflected from buildings, hills, or other large objects. Repositioning or rotating the antenna can often improve the picture.

<PIC>

4. Improve reception in weak-signal areas

If the picture contains many small dots or looks snowy, the TV may be located in a weak signal area. In this case, you may need to reposition the outdoor antenna, use a higher-quality antenna cable, or install a more suitable outdoor antenna to improve the signal.

<PIC>

Additional tips:
- If the stored channels have poor picture quality, run AUTO PROGRAM again after adjusting the antenna.
- Make sure the antenna cable is firmly connected to the 75 Ohm antenna jack.
- Check for local interference from nearby electrical equipment, large metal objects, or other signal-blocking sources.
- If certain channels still cannot be tuned, adjust the antenna and run AUTO PROGRAM again.,['Manual35_39', 'Manual35_5', 'Manual35_7', 'television0_02', 'television0_03']

For the best picture and sound, connect the DVD player using the TV’s COMPONENT DVD INPUT (480i) and the matching left/right audio inputs. Before connecting, turn off the TV and DVD player, and match the cable colors/labels carefully.

1. Connect the DVD player’s component video outputs to the TV’s COMPONENT DVD INPUT (480i):
   - DVD Y → TV Y
   - DVD Pb / PB / Cb / B-Y → TV PB
   - DVD Pr / PR / Cr / R-Y → TV PR

2. Connect the DVD player’s audio outputs to the TV’s audio inputs:
   - DVD L audio output → TV L audio IN
   - DVD R audio output → TV R audio IN

<PIC>

3. If your DVD player uses different names for the component video terminals, connect them by function rather than by wording:
   - Y always connects to Y
   - Cb / B-Y / Pb / PB connects to PB
   - Cr / R-Y / Pr / PR connects to PR

<PIC>

4. If you want sound through an external audio system instead of the TV speakers, connect the DVD player’s L/R audio outputs to the audio system’s matching L/R audio inputs, then select the correct input on the audio system.

5. Turn on the TV, then press the TV/VIDEO button on the remote control or on the TV front panel until COMPONENT is selected.

6. Turn on the DVD player and start playback. If there is no picture, recheck that the TV is set to COMPONENT and that Y/PB/PR are connected to the matching jacks. If there is picture but no sound, recheck the L/R audio cables and the selected audio input.

Note: The TV remembers the last input mode used, even after it is turned off.,['Manual35_43', 'Manual35_44']

To effectively troubleshoot technical issues or malfunctions, you should first verify the power supply and all physical connections to ensure the device is receiving adequate energy and is properly linked to necessary peripherals. Next, inspect the product for any visible physical damage or environmental factors such as overheating that might affect performance. It is also crucial to confirm that the software or firmware is updated to the latest version and that all internal settings are correctly configured according to the user manual. Performing a standard system restart or a factory reset often resolves temporary glitches, and you should always check for specific error codes or indicator lights that can point to the root cause of the malfunction. If the problem persists after these checks, please provide the specific symptoms so I can assist you further or guide you to our technical support team for a more detailed diagnosis.

To ensure your safety and the smooth completion of the process, please strictly follow these precautions: first, thoroughly read the product manual and follow the specific step-by-step instructions provided for your model; ensure the operating environment is dry, well-ventilated, and free from any flammable materials or hazards; always use the appropriate protective equipment such as gloves or safety glasses if required by the task; verify that the power supply is stable and all connections are secure before starting; keep the equipment out of reach of children and pets during use; if you notice any unusual heat, noise, or smell, please stop the operation immediately and disconnect the power; finally, if you are unsure about any specific step, do not hesitate to contact our technical support team for professional guidance.

- Scrubbing Feedback
- Brush head replacement reminder

Please note: if Pressure Sensor Feedback is deactivated, Adaptive Intensity will also be deactivated.

There are two ways to adjust these settings: through the app or directly from the toothbrush handle.

## 1. Adjusting features from the app

The following features can be activated or deactivated in the app:

- Adaptive Intensity
- Scrubbing Feedback

To connect your toothbrush to the app:

1. Download the app to your phone.
2. Make sure Bluetooth is turned on.
3. Pick up your toothbrush so it becomes active and the lights turn on.
4. Open the app.
5. Follow the guided steps in the app.
6. Pair your toothbrush with the app.

Once paired, you can customize the available toothbrush settings from the app.

## 2. Adjusting features from the toothbrush handle

You can also activate or deactivate selected features directly from the handle.

### Step 1: Place the handle on the charging stand

Make sure the toothbrush handle is properly seated on the charging stand before starting.

### Step 2: Press and hold the power button

Press and hold the power button for different lengths of time depending on which feature you want to adjust:

<PIC>

- Adaptive Intensity  
  Hold the power button for up to 3 seconds.  
  You will hear 1 beep, and the SenseIQ indicator will light up for 3 seconds.

- Pressure Sensor Feedback  
  Hold the power button for up to 5 seconds.  
  You will hear 1 beep followed by 2 beeps, and the SenseIQ indicator plus the light ring will light up purple for 3 seconds.

- Brush head replacement reminder  
  Hold the power button for up to 7 seconds.  
  You will hear 1 beep, then 2 beeps, then 3 beeps, and the brush head replacement reminder indicator will light up for 3 seconds.

After selecting the feature, check the battery indicator and tones to confirm whether the feature was activated or deactivated:

<PIC>

- Activated: the battery indicator blinks white 3 times, and you hear 3 tones from low to high.
- Deactivated: the battery indicator blinks amber 3 times, and you hear 3 tones from high to low.

In short, place the handle on the charger, hold the power button until the signal for the desired feature appears, then use the battery indicator color and tone pattern to confirm whether that feature has been turned on or off.,['Manual37_13', 'toothbrush0_11']

To charge the electric toothbrush using the charging travel case, please follow these steps:

1. Connect the USB-C cord to the charging travel case, then connect the other end to the USB wall adapter.

<PIC>

2. Plug the USB wall adapter into an electrical outlet.

3. Place the toothbrush handle into the travel case correctly. If you are also storing the brush head, place it in its designated slot in the case.

<PIC>

4. Confirm that charging has started properly. When the toothbrush is positioned correctly and the case is powered:
- The toothbrush will beep twice.
- The lights on the handle will illuminate in an upward motion.
- While charging, the battery indicator will blink white.

<PIC>

5. Leave the travel case plugged in until the toothbrush is fully charged. When charging is complete, the battery light will stop blinking and turn off.

<PIC>

For better stability while charging, place the travel case on its side as shown above.

Battery indicator while the handle is in the travel case:
- Charging: the battery indicator blinks white.
- Fully charged: the indicator lights white for about 30 seconds, then turns off.

<PIC>

Safety reminders:
- Only use the compatible USB wall adapter, charging base, and charging travel case for charging.
- Charge, use, and store the toothbrush between 0°C and 40°C.
- If the product becomes unusually hot, gives off an unusual smell, changes color, or takes much longer than normal to charge, stop using and charging it and contact customer support.,['Manual37_18', 'Manual37_19', 'Manual37_20', 'Manual37_21', 'Manual37_22']
